reviews.author: Riali.
reviews.content:
I used to use this app for a while and it worked okay, but stopped working out and tried to redownload it.

Why. Is. There. Such. A. Strong. Emphasis. On. Weight. Loss!?

You can’t even log in and use the app without setting a weight loss goal! Every single exercise comes with a note on how much weight you’ll lose. I understand it’s useful if you ARE trying to lose weight, but the app SPECIFICALLY ASKS what your goals are (disappointed to find out that increasing flexibility wasn’t an option, but I guess it’s a little niche) and never once did I include weight loss. 

I think it’s toxic (and this is more critique of diet culture as whole rather than the app itself) to assume every goddamn person tying to stay fit is trying to lose weight. I just want to be healthy, not feel pressured into losing weight when I’m already at a healthy one.

And, in the end, I don’t particularly care too much, I just deleted the app again, but what about the teenagers using this app? (There is a specific option when you log in to state you are under 18.) I don’t think it’s healthy to subject teenagers to that constant pressure, or worse, someone who has already struggled with EDs or an obsession with weight loss/appearance.

It’s one thing for the option to be there, in fact, I think it’s great for those who need or want it, but the way that this is constantly shoved down your throat is ridiculous. The creators are (or at should be) fully aware that losing weight isn’t the only reason to work out, so why don’t they act like it?
reviews.title: Why is there such a string focus on losing weight?

reviews.author: RLeighA
reviews.content: 2nd attempt at reviewing this dogwater app. They’re locking previously-free features behind paywalls and ads. I’ve been using it for over a year but this is what ends my streak for good. You used to be able to change your personalized fitness routine whenever, to accommodate your needs—now it’s locked behind a subscription. There used to be a decent number of free workouts—now they’re locked behind NINETY SECONDS of ads. Every one I’ve tried so far has gotten a pop-up “watch 90 seconds of ads to get this workout free!” BRO IT WAS ALREADY FREE. IT WAS FREE BEFORE YOU STUCK FORCED ADS IN FRONT OF IT WHAT DO YOU MEAN ‘get it free with some ads’ IT WAS ALREADY FREE. And if you don’t have the “option” for ads available? YOU JUST CAN’T DO THE WORKOUT!!! And if for any reason the app resets? You guessed it: you have to watch another ad to restart the workout. Meanwhile FitOn not only has no forced ads, it also saves your progress, so if there are any technical issues, you can pick up right where you left off. I will never support an app that strips away features just to lock them behind $$$. Neither should you. Look at spotify. You used to be able to listen to whatever you wanted and skip as much as you wanted, but people kept using it after they took those things away. Turned the app into Freemium garbage. People keep using it. They keep taking features away and demanding you pay for it. STOP SUPPORTING SCUMMY PRACTICES. FitOn is free. Workout tor Women has a bunch of great free workouts. FitnessOnline even lets you make your own workout routine! Stop supporting this app until they backtrack and stop shoving ads down our throats. They WILL continue to chip away at what little free content remains until everything is plastered with ads or locked behind a subscription. Look around and see how many other companies have done the same and gotten away with minimal pushback. Stop rolling over and letting them. Previously 7/10, now absolute 0/10 will never recommend again unless they backtrack on this within the next couple of updates. I am NOT going to stop screaming about this. I am SO TIRED OF ADS INVADING LITERALLY EVERYTHING. THEY DO NOT WORK. THEY ARE A CANCER AND A PLAGUE AND MAKE EVERYTHING WORSE.
reviews.title: Cannot recommend this anymore

reviews.author: *fart fart*
reviews.content: Some workouts are great even without the paid premium. It has workouts, yoga, stretches and, etc. You’re able to move your focus to different parts of your body but if there was a better way to narrow down to specifics that would be great. Although lately I have been feeling unmotivated to use this app for my workouts and yoga even. I am currently very much pregnant and about to pop but during the beginning of my pregnancy I was on my workouts but I wanted to focus on prenatal workouts to get my body ready for this baby. So I switched up my settings (apparently pregnancy is a special needs) and went searching for workouts. I found the prenatal program and I tried it out. I was about 17-18 weeks at that point and I was getting my baby bump. Tell me why their prenatal workouts/yoga have you laying on your stomach doing single leg raises or supermans. Like wouldn’t they know that pregnant women shouldn’t be putting pressure on your stomach?? Apparently not… but I haven’t used this app since June and I’m writing this in October trying out my old workouts and yoga since I never liked working out on my stomach but I need to get the ball rolling since I’m expecting to have my baby soon. It’s just crazy how a women’s workout app doesn’t even accommodate to pregnant women as much as you would expect them to. Very strange but simple fix yeah? I get you can skip the parts that you don’t want to do but I hate having to stop my groove just to run up and and tap on my screen when there should be no need to skip a stomach workout on a prenatal program.
reviews.title: Pros and cons
